title: ‘Hamlet’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of Hamlet, Prince of Denmark, often shortened to Hamlet, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare sometime between 1599 and 1601.

title: ‘Macbeth’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of Macbeth, often shortened to Macbeth, is a tragedy by William Shakespeare, estimated to have been first performed in 1606.

title: ‘The Importance of Being Earnest’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Oscar Wilde
fact: The Importance of Being Earnest, a Trivial Comedy for Serious People is a play by Oscar Wilde, the last of his four drawing-room plays, following Lady Windermere's Fan (1892), A Woman of No Importance (1893) and An Ideal Husband (1895).

title: ‘A Midsummer Night's Dream’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: A Midsummer Night's Dream is a comedy play written by William Shakespeare in about 1595 or 1596.

title: ‘King Lear’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of King Lear, often shortened to King Lear, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare in late 1605 or early 1606.

title: ‘Othello’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tragedy of Othello, the Moor of Venice, often shortened to Othello, is a tragedy written by William Shakespeare around 1603.

title: ‘The Tempest’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Tempest is a play by William Shakespeare, probably written in 1610–1611, and thought to be one of the last plays that he wrote alone.

title: ‘Twelfth Night’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Twelfth Night, or What You Will is a romantic comedy by William Shakespeare, believed to have been written around 1601–1602 as a Twelfth Night entertainment for the close of the Christmas season.

title: ‘Much Ado About Nothing’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Much Ado About Nothing is a comedy by William Shakespeare thought to have been written in 1598 and 1599.

title: ‘The Merchant of Venice’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Merchant of Venice is a play by William Shakespeare, believed to have been written between 1596 and 1598.

title: ‘Antigone’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Sophocles
fact: Antigone is an Athenian tragedy written by Sophocles in either 442 or 440 BC and first performed at the Festival of Dionysus of the same year.

title: ‘Oedipus Rex’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Sophocles
fact: Oedipus Rex, also known by its Greek title, Oedipus Tyrannus, or Oedipus the King, is an Athenian tragedy written by Sophocles.

title: ‘As You Like It’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: As You Like It is a pastoral comedy by William Shakespeare believed to have been written in 1599 and first published in the First Folio in 1623.

title: ‘Hedda Gabler’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: Hedda Gabler is a play written by Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen.

title: ‘A Doll's House’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: A Doll's House is a three-act play written by Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen.

title: ‘Pygmalion’ premieres
subtitle: Play by George Bernard Shaw
fact: Pygmalion is a play written by Bernard Shaw in 1912, named after the Greek mythological figure.

title: ‘Peer Gynt’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: Peer Gynt is a five-act play in verse written in 1867 by the Norwegian dramatist Henrik Ibsen.

title: ‘Oresteia’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aeschylus
fact: The Oresteia is a trilogy of Greek tragedies written by Aeschylus in the 5th century BC, concerning the murder of Agamemnon by Clytemnestra, the murder of Clytemnestra by Orestes, the trial of Orestes, the end of the curse on the House of Atreus, and the pacification of the Furies.

title: ‘Uncle Vanya’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Anton Chekhov
fact: Uncle Vanya is a play by the Russian playwright Anton Chekhov.

title: ‘The Seagull’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Anton Chekhov
fact: The Seagull is a play by Russian dramatist Anton Chekhov, written in 1895 and first produced in 1896.

title: ‘Antony and Cleopatra’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Antony and Cleopatra is a tragedy by William Shakespeare.

title: ‘Lysistrata’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: Lysistrata is an ancient Greek comedy by Aristophanes, first staged in early 411 BCE at Lenaea festival in classical Athens.

title: ‘The Winter's Tale’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Winter's Tale is a play by William Shakespeare originally published in the First Folio of 1623.

title: ‘The Bacchae’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: The Bacchae is an ancient Greek tragedy, written by the Athenian playwright Euripides during his final years in Macedonia, at the court of Archelaus I of Macedon.

title: ‘Coriolanus’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Coriolanus is a tragedy by William Shakespeare, believed to have been written between 1605 and 1608.

title: ‘R.U.R.’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Karel Čapek
fact: R.U.R. is a 1920 science fiction play by the Czech writer Karel Čapek.

title: ‘An Enemy of the People’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: An Enemy of the People is an 1882 play by Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen that explores the conflict between personal integrity and societal norms.

title: ‘The Cherry Orchard’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Anton Chekhov
fact: The Cherry Orchard is the last play by the Russian playwright Anton Chekhov.

title: ‘Three Sisters’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Anton Chekhov
fact: Three Sisters is a play by the Russian author and playwright Anton Chekhov.

title: ‘The Comedy of Errors’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Comedy of Errors is one of William Shakespeare's earliest plays.

title: ‘Henry IV, Part 1’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Henry IV, Part 1 is a play by William Shakespeare, probably written in the mid-1590s and first published in quarto in 1598.

title: ‘Richard II’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Life and Death of King Richard the Second (1595), also Richard II, is a Shakespearean history play about the lifetime and reign of King Richard II of England.

title: ‘Measure for Measure’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Measure for Measure is a play by William Shakespeare, believed to have been written in 1603 or 1604 and first performed in 1604.

title: ‘Ghosts’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: Ghosts is a play by the Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen.

title: ‘Love's Labour's Lost’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Love's Labour's Lost is one of William Shakespeare's early comedies, believed to have been written in the mid-1590s for a performance at the Inns of Court before Queen Elizabeth I.

title: ‘Tartuffe’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: Tartuffe, or The Impostor, or The Hypocrite, first performed in 1664, is a theatrical comedy by Molière.

title: ‘The Merry Wives of Windsor’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Merry Wives of Windsor or Sir John Falstaff and the Merry Wives of Windsor is a comedy by William Shakespeare first published in 1602, though believed to have been written in or before 1597.

title: ‘Cymbeline’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Cymbeline, also known as The Tragedie of Cymbeline or Cymbeline, King of Britain, is a play by William Shakespeare set in Ancient Britain and based on legends that formed part of the Matter of Britain concerning the early historical Celtic British King Cunobeline.

title: ‘All's Well That Ends Well’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: All's Well That Ends Well is a play by William Shakespeare, published in the First Folio in 1623, where it is listed among the comedies.

title: ‘Prometheus Bound’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aeschylus
fact: Prometheus Bound is an ancient Greek tragedy traditionally attributed to Aeschylus and thought to have been composed sometime between 479 BC and the terminus ante quem of 424 BC.

title: ‘King John’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Life and Death of King John, by William Shakespeare, is a history play about the reign of John, King of England, the son of Henry II and Eleanor of Aquitaine, and the father of Henry III.

title: ‘An Ideal Husband’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Oscar Wilde
fact: An Ideal Husband is a four-act play by Oscar Wilde that revolves around blackmail and political corruption, and touches on the themes of public and private honour.

title: ‘The Frogs’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: The Frogs is a comedy written by the Ancient Greek playwright Aristophanes.

title: ‘Henry VI, Part 1’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Henry VI, Part 1, often referred to as 1 Henry VI, is a history play by William Shakespeare—possibly in collaboration with Thomas Nashe and others—believed to have been written in 1591.

title: ‘The Master Builder’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: The Master Builder is a play by Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen.

title: ‘The Misanthrope’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: The Misanthrope, or the Cantankerous Lover is a 17th-century comedy of manners in verse written by Molière.

title: ‘Henry VIII’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Famous History of the Life of King Henry the Eighth, often shortened to Henry VIII, is a collaborative history play, written by William Shakespeare and John Fletcher, based on the life of Henry VIII.

title: ‘Saint Joan’ premieres
subtitle: Play by George Bernard Shaw
fact: Saint Joan is a play by George Bernard Shaw about the 15th-century French military figure Joan of Arc.

title: ‘The Clouds’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: The Clouds is a Greek comedy play written by the playwright Aristophanes.

title: ‘Timon of Athens’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Life of Tymon of Athens, often shortened to Timon of Athens, is a play written by William Shakespeare and likely also Thomas Middleton in about 1606.

title: ‘Pericles, Prince of Tyre’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Pericles, Prince of Tyre is a Jacobean play written at least in part by William Shakespeare and included in modern editions of his collected works despite questions over its authorship, as it was not included in the First Folio.

title: ‘The Trojan Women’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: The Trojan Women is a tragedy by the Greek playwright Euripides, produced in 415 BCE.

title: ‘Oedipus at Colonus’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Sophocles
fact: Oedipus at Colonus is the second of the three Theban plays of the Athenian tragedian Sophocles.

title: ‘Electra’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Sophocles
fact: Electra, is a Greek tragedy by Sophocles.

title: ‘The Wild Duck’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: The Wild Duck is an 1884 play by the Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen.

title: ‘Iphigenia in Aulis’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Iphigenia in Aulis or Iphigenia at Aulis is the last of the extant works by the playwright Euripides.

title: ‘Henry IV, Part 2’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Henry IV, Part 2 is a history play by William Shakespeare believed to have been written between 1596 and 1599.

title: ‘Le Bourgeois gentilhomme’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: Le Bourgeois gentilhomme is a five-act comédie-ballet — a play with music, dance and singing — first performed on 14 October 1670 for the court of Louis XIV at the Château of Chambord.

title: ‘The Miser’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: The Miser is a five-act comedy in prose by the French playwright Molière.

title: ‘The Birds’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: The Birds is a comedy by the Ancient Greek playwright Aristophanes.

title: ‘The Persians’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aeschylus
fact: The Persians is an ancient Greek tragedy written during the Classical period of Ancient Greece by the Greek tragedian Aeschylus.

title: ‘The Lady from the Sea’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Henrik Ibsen
fact: The Lady from the Sea is a play written in 1888 by Norwegian playwright Henrik Ibsen inspired by the ballad Agnete og Havmanden.

title: ‘Phèdre’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Jean Racine
fact: Phèdre is a French dramatic tragedy in five acts written in alexandrine verse by Jean Racine, first performed in 1677 at the theatre of the Hôtel de Bourgogne in Paris.

title: ‘Assemblywomen’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: Assemblywomen is a comedy written by the Greek playwright Aristophanes in 391 BCE.

title: ‘The Two Noble Kinsmen’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: The Two Noble Kinsmen is a Jacobean tragicomedy, first published in 1634 and attributed jointly to John Fletcher and William Shakespeare.

title: ‘Henry VI, Part 3’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Henry VI, Part 3 is a history play by William Shakespeare believed to have been written in 1591 and set during the lifetime of King Henry VI of England.

title: ‘Hippolytus’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Hippolytus is an Ancient Greek tragedy by Euripides, based on the myth of Hippolytus, son of Theseus.

title: ‘Ajax’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Sophocles
fact: Sophocles' Ajax, or Aias, is a Greek tragedy written in the 5th century BCE.

title: ‘Henry VI, Part 2’ premieres
subtitle: Play by William Shakespeare
fact: Henry VI, Part 2 (1591) is a Shakespearean history play about King Henry VI of England's inability to quell the bickering of his noblemen, the death of his trusted advisor Humphrey, Duke of Gloucester, and the political rise of Richard of York, 3rd Duke of York; it culminates with the First Battle of St Albans (1455), the initial battle of the Wars of the Roses, which were civil wars between the House of Lancaster and the House of York.

title: ‘The Imaginary Invalid’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: The Imaginary Invalid, The Hypochondriac, or The Would-Be Invalid is a three-act comédie-ballet by the French playwright Molière with dance sequences and musical interludes by Marc-Antoine Charpentier.

title: ‘Alcestis’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Alcestis is an Athenian tragedy by the ancient Greek playwright Euripides.

title: ‘Don Juan’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: Dom Juan ou le Festin de Pierre is a five-act 1665 comedy by Molière based upon the Spanish legend of Don Juan Tenorio.

title: ‘Hecuba’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Hecuba is a tragedy by Euripides, written c.

title: ‘Cyclops’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Cyclops is an ancient Greek satyr play by Euripides, based closely on an episode from the Odyssey.

title: ‘Iphigenia in Tauris’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Iphigenia in Tauris is a drama by the playwright Euripides, written between 414 BC and 412 BC.

title: ‘Thesmophoriazusae’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: Thesmophoriazusae, or Women at the Thesmophoria, is one of eleven surviving comedy plays by Aristophanes.

title: ‘Suppliants’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aeschylus
fact: The Suppliants, also called The Suppliant Maidens, The Suppliant Women, or Supplices is a play by Aeschylus.

title: ‘The Knights’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: The Knights was the fourth play written by Aristophanes, who is considered the master of Old Comedy.

title: ‘The Wasps’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: The Wasps is the fourth in chronological order of the eleven surviving plays by Aristophanes.

title: ‘Orestes’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Orestes (408 BCE) is an Ancient Greek play by Euripides that follows the events of Orestes after he had murdered his mother.

title: ‘Herakles’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Herakles is an Athenian tragedy by Euripides that was first performed c.

title: ‘Helen’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Helen is a drama by Euripides about Helen, first produced in 412 BC for the Dionysia in a trilogy that also contained Euripides' lost Andromeda.

title: ‘Philoctetes’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Sophocles
fact: Philoctetes is a play by Sophocles.

title: ‘Electra’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Euripides' Electra is a tragedy probably written in the mid 410s BC, likely before 413 BC.

title: ‘Boris Godunov’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Alexander Pushkin
fact: Boris Godunov is a play by Alexander Pushkin.

title: ‘Trachiniae’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Sophocles
fact: Women of Trachis or The Trachiniae c.

title: ‘Andromaque’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Jean Racine
fact: Andromaque is a tragedy in five acts by the French playwright Jean Racine written in alexandrine verse.

title: ‘Andromache’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Andromache is an Athenian tragedy by Euripides.

title: ‘Ion’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Ion is an ancient Greek play by Euripides, thought to have been written between 414 and 412 BC.

title: ‘The School for Wives’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: The School for Wives is a theatrical comedy written by the seventeenth-century French playwright Molière and considered by some critics to be one of his finest achievements.

title: ‘Seven against Thebes’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aeschylus
fact: Seven Against Thebes is the third play in an Oedipus-themed trilogy produced by Aeschylus in 467 BC.

title: ‘Les Femmes Savantes’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: Les Femmes savantes is a comedy by Molière in five acts, written in verse.

title: ‘The Phoenician Women’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: The Phoenician Women is a tragedy by Euripides, based on the same story as Aeschylus' play Seven Against Thebes.

title: ‘Peace’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Aristophanes
fact: Peace is an Athenian Old Comedy written and produced by the Greek playwright Aristophanes.

title: ‘Herakles' Children’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Euripides
fact: Children of Heracles is an Athenian tragedy written by Euripides.

title: ‘Scapin the Schemer’ premieres
subtitle: Play by Molière
fact: Scapin the Schemer is a three-act comedy of intrigue by the French playwright Molière.
